title: synaedrys pruinosa
side_effects: Synaedrys pruinosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, or even respiratory issues in some individuals. Ingesting the plant's parts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or essential oils may result in headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Improper handling or preparation of Synaedrys pruinosa can also cause eye and skin burns, emphasizing the importance of proper handling and caution.
